events.description: I recently got diagnosed over Christmas and have just started Acetazolamide. I also have ADHD and it’s frustrating me a lot on days I feel debilitated by side effects. I hope these improve in time but thought I’d join the programme to help me process and stay positive.
My daughter 💕
@Kraven a lovely photo. I, too, am very grateful for my sons. How old is your daughter?
For a pain free day out with friends, even in the pouring rain 🤣
I am going to do my Rockfit aerobics class at least once this week. on In the comfort of my living room.. I will aim to do this Once.

A pain-free day and time with friends — even in pouring rain — that sounds like a real win! ☔🦜 There’s something extra special about enjoying yourself despite the weather. I’m so glad you had that kind of day to hold onto 💛

This is brilliant 🤩🙌 especially knowing it was a struggle to get started. That says a lot about your determination. I love that you noticed you were glad afterwards too — that’s really powerful feedback from your own body and mind.

If you do decide to fit another one in, make sure it works with your energy rather than against it. Well done you 🤘🏻🍫

My two are 14 and 22, time goes far too fast; however, my eldest has severe learning difficulties, so is in many ways younger and still loves cuddles! 

I hope the trip to the zoo was enjoyable regardless. Remember, to be able to look after your daughter, you need to look after yourself. I know how hard it is.
For my friends, and all of the adventures we’ve been sharing together for years.
Is that dungeons and dragons? I have always wanted to have a go; my son used to play in a club at school. It's great that you have supportive friends to share your time with 😊
@Emma_R hello 👋🏻absolutely! I’m a single parent of an adhd child too and it’s half term 😩🤣. Today is our guilt free lazy day lol.
I really enjoyed this activity and found it very calming. It reminded me of my trip to Cornwall last year and bathing my feet in the River Valency in Boscastle. A very happy memory.
To re-dye my hair. on At home, likely next week.. I will aim to do this Every 6ish weeks.
A really great achievable goal. I, too, would like to dye my hair this week, though I would like my hair cut first lol. What colour are you choosing? 😊
To volunteer at the local community garden. on The local community garden on Thursday mornings.. I will aim to do this Once a week.

For my mum. She really is the best. I’ve had a tough few days from Acetazolamide side effects, and today I barely had to lift a finger. When I feel exhausted and just awful, it’s been really appreciated.

@kristy2 A herbalism course sounds really interesting. Is it something you will do in person locally or online? A really positive goal, I agree, getting outside helps my mental health too. I shall look forward to finding out more about how your volunteering is going 😊.

For my little Raven. The most intelligent kitty I’ve ever co-habited with.
She loves, she also love bites, she basically suffers no fools 🤣. She can open doors, she also plays fetch like a dog and runs to the door to tell me company is coming, that is if she’s not surveying her kingdom from our balcony ledge like it’s Pride Rock lol! But mostly she makes me laugh daily especially when her and my daughter play together, it’s wonderful to see.

This is so lovely @kristy2 🤗 When you’re feeling wiped out from side effects, having someone step in and just take over a bit can mean everything. It sounds like your mum really showed up for you. I’m so glad you felt supported and cared for when you needed it most 💛

This made me smile so much as my cat (Harry) is the same 🐈‍⬛ Raven sounds like an absolute character — clever, confident, and completely in charge of her kingdom! I love that she keeps you laughing 😅 There’s something really special about the joy pets bring, especially seeing her and your daughter play together. What a lovely thing to feel grateful for 🙌
For Spring 🥰 I’ve had 2 wonderful days so far being able to get outside, it’s lifted my mood.
A haircut! on Next Wednesday at the salon.. I will aim to do this When it needs it lol!

It seems a silly thing but I’m grateful to fit into jeans I haven’t been able to get in for 2+ years. It may not be how I intended to lose weight this year (I don’t recommend fear as a motivator), but I’m now just over a stone and half down in my weight from eating better healthier choices and I cried so much when the zip went up. I’m not sure where that reaction came from, maybe years of pushing things down in order to survive, but I sure needed it. So today I’m grateful for the biggest hug my little girl gave me during this moment x
Gardening - to plant some Rosemary and Basil on my balcony  on My kitchen/balcony.. I will aim to do this Watering when needed.
To paint or draw something  on At home by the window.. I will aim to do this Once a week.

My sister is getting married in July. I hope I’m able to assist the photographer with wedding photography on the day as planned. I’m terrified of my vision changing so found this task quite hard to do this week. My biggest hope is to be able to see my daughter continue to grow. This is her flower girl basket for the day that I glammed up from when my sister was a flower girl. Memories are so important so that’s my biggest hope, to not miss seeing any of it x

I’m grateful for my family for driving to a spiritual event today and for the lady who gave me a reading and helped realign my focus. Thankfully mercury retrograde is over and while my body is suffering a little now, things feel lifted and brighter today 🥰. x
To enrol in a Herbal Medicine course on Online, self paced with help of a mentor.. I will aim to do this It should take me 12-18 months, starting next week

For my sister who visited recently and made us walk to the beach. For my daughter for learning more patience in her Easter holiday. For my mum and her partner for supporting and feeding us all weekend without complaints when I had a few bad days of discomfort. xx

events.description:
This is my third time (I think) joining the course. 

Firstly, I’d like to finish it. I think my ADHD (only recently suggested as a diagnosis) may be partially at blame for that. 

But also I just go through massive ups and downs with this frequently. At the moment, I seem to be stockpiling conditions and it feels like I’m not getting any medical support and it’s too hard to advocate for myself.
So I guess I’m hoping for confidence and energy/support in managing it all better because the fatigue and overwhelm is crippling sometimes.

That I got home safely with Corby from our week in London as the weather was awful, my leg was in plain, and I wasn’t feeling great so didn’t want to drive but actually despite the extra concentration needed because of the bad weather, the journey wasn’t too difficult or delayed.
To lose weight (1 stone) on At home through calorie counting . I will aim to do this Every day
Actually complete this course this time  on In the evening after work for up to 20 mins. . I will aim to do this Every day.

That sounds like such a relief. Driving in awful weather when you’re already not feeling great and in pain takes a lot of focus and resilience. I love that you’ve recognised that although it felt daunting, you managed it and it wasn’t as bad as you feared. Safe, home, and through it — that’s definitely something to be grateful for 💛

@nikki30 this is a clear goal, and it’s great you’ve planned ways to help yourself. With a confidence of 5, maybe think about one small thing that could nudge it closer to 6 or 7 — even tiny steps count! 🪜

@nikki30 this is a really thoughtful goal — breaking it into small sections and using reminders sounds smart. With a confidence of 6, maybe think about one tiny tweak that could nudge it closer to 7 — even small steps are wins, and noticing them counts! 💛

Today I had a financial crisis due to a delay in payment for my work meaning that I was going to be short of money for two weeks when I needed it bills etc. I asked a friend for help to cover me for two weeks and they came through for me immediately so I’m incredibly grateful for the friends I have. I may not see them very often and wish they lived closer but they are there for me when I need them.

@nikki30 that must have felt really stressful at first. What a relief that your friend was able to step in so quickly  It says so much about the strength of your friendships that you felt able to ask, and that they were there when it mattered. Even if you don’t see them often, that kind of support is incredibly special 🙂

@nikki30 that makes so much sense so thank you for that 💛

We don’t need forced optimism here but a 6 is honest. And honest goals are strong foundations. 🌿

Yesterday was another bed day and I struggled to stay awake. It was making me upset because I had things I needed to do - particularly work-wise. However, with the exception of 2 hrs of my day, I had this ball of fluff next to me at all times keeping me company and quite happily letting me hug him like a teddy bear all day. So I’m grateful for him and his incredible temperament - especially the way he sticks extra close when I’m at my lowest.

Related to my last post, I’m grateful that I found my dogwalker Jade. I eventually reconciled that I couldn’t walk Corby as much as I wanted/he needed and had previously met Jade on the beach and connected via Insta. I was so worried that Corby wouldn’t want to go for a walk with someone else but he adores her and can’t wait for her to come pick him up. Plus he gets to walk with lots of different friends on his walks so it’s great enrichment for him. 

Jade is also a dog trainer and behaviourist and has been helping me work with Corby on his Assistance Dog training. This has been so helpful so I’m incredibly grateful for her and everything she brings to our lives.
To leave for London calm and prepared at the time I said I would with just the important items.  on I’m going to pack before Saturday . I will aim to do this Every night after work

I emphasise with bed days, please be kind with yourself. Whatever the reason, keeping you in bed, you needed it. 

I am so pleased you had a beautiful fur baby to comfort you. 😊

@nikki30  I am so pleased you found someone to help, and that both yourself and Corby get on well with her. I emphasise how difficult it is to accept that you need support. I have had these struggles myself. I find it helps to think about what you would say to a friend who has the same issues.
Such a beautiful picture. I look forward to finding out if you succeeded in travelling when planned. Enjoy your time with your friend 😊

(I apologise in advance that my gratitudes largely revolve around my dog. I got him just after my partner died because I needed an outlet for my love and caring - and because I was alone in the town I live in. So he is still very much my world just over a year later) 


This gratitude is related to my dog’s breeder, Lynn. She recommended Corby from the remaining puppies in the litter as being most suited to my lifestyle and needs. 

At the time, I thought she was just appeasing me because I should have had a wider selection than I did, but she had promised some of the remaining puppies to others out of order as it were. 

However, she was so right and he is perfect. And more important, perfect for me. Fun, clever, mischievous- but 100% attuned and bonded to me. Able to temper his playfulness around my health and work from the start AND trained himself to perform assistance tasks to assist my PTSD. 

After just being woken gently by Corby for the second time from nightmares tonight, I’m very grateful for that and also know that not all dogs would behave that way so am grateful for Lynn picking out Corby for me. 

Photo is of Lynn holding Corby the day I picked him up.
Relating to the below - I’m grateful to Corby again for consistently looking after me - even though I never taught him to do that.

I made it out last night for the one consistent social thing I do - which is to do the monthly quiz at a local pub. I never want to go, I’m always too exhausted, but I’m always glad afterwards that I pushed through even though I have to take extra care the next day to recover. 

So I’m grateful to my teammate, Abi, who was a couple of years ago was a stranger who posted on a local Facebook group looking to make up a quiz team as she was new to the area. Without her, I wouldn’t push to do this every month.

@nikki30 Corby is gorgeous, and you certainly don't need to apologise for him being your primary gratitude. I love seeing fur baby pictures 😊 I am so pleased you have bonded perfectly and that Lynn's instinct was correct.
@nikki30 Such a beautiful picture 😊 A lovely gratitude to have

@nikki30 I am really pleased you made it out to the quiz, well done. Pacing is something we cover later in the course that might be helpful for you. 

Such a heartwarming story of how you made a friend and quiz companion 😊
Complete PIP Mandatory Reconsideration paperwork on Today and tomorrow at home. I will aim to do this Until it’s done. It needs a long focus session

events.description: I’ve found it difficult to accept that having iih flare ups and/or medication could be for forever… I am hoping after this course my feelings can settle and I can start to accept this as the norm rather than it feel as though it’s hanging over me. It will also be nice to connect with others who have IIH and understand that I may look fine physically but inside I am not okay!
My cat Mama - my little bestie. Here she is after playing with some catnip.
Minimum 30 minute walk around the bay  on Wednesday morning . I will aim to do this Once then change location
Aww, she is gorgeous. 😊How old is she?

@lucy94 I, too, hope it is a nice day for you. Is it today that you planned to have your walk? Remember, things happen, such as bad weather, be kind to yourself. If you don't complete your goal, you can amend or cancel it.
To finish a cross stitch kit that I had as a Christmas gift  on I will work on it at home in the evening. I will aim to do this I will do a little bit each night if i can

This morning I went for breakfast at a restaurant I used to visit quite often but now it’s too expensive and I can’t justify it. My mother in law bought us a gift voucher for Christmas - I am grateful to have in-laws who listen and care about me and know me so well - it was a great gift!
@lucy94 I love your goal, it would be lovely to see a work-in-progress pic. I found a cross stitch in a drawer I was sorting through with an aim to get rid of stuff, yesterday...I did a bit lol.
Yummy @lucy94! 🤩 Being able to enjoy somewhere you love, without the guilt or worry about cost, sounds like a real treat, and a lovely reminder of how cared for you are 💛

@lucy94 it sounds like you made the most of the morning despite the weather! 🌧️ Even though the walk was shorter than planned, you still enjoyed time out, coffee, and a chat — that’s a win in itself! 🙌 Sometimes it’s about the small moments rather than the full plan, and it sounds like you made it work.
Repot 3 of my house plants, tidy them up and move them to better locations.  on At home . I will aim to do this Once

I would like to start pottering in the garden - mulching, repotting, weeding and start thinking about which seeds I will be planting in the next 2 weeks.  on It will be done at home. . I will aim to do this Here and there when the weather is dry.
To go for a walk - minimum of 30 mins  on At local park/fields . I will aim to do this At least once

Im grateful for my 3 beautiful children they gove me purpose to keep going on bad days.
To get out the house and do a activity with my kids. on Go bowling during the week. I will aim to do this Once

Hopefully this weekend i can its a nightmare when trying to stay awake but your eyes are trying to shut.But tommorows a new day xx
I found this very relaxing and carming to the point it made me sleepy.Im going to practice this every other night ..
Im going to do some core exercises. on I will do it in the living room every other night.. I will aim to do this Every other night

I am grateful to be here today and to be able to do this course .

Im going to complete a tumbler cup full of rhinestones . on I will do it from home in my living room .. I will aim to do this Every evening .

events.description: I am going to start walking with my 2 pugs. on Daily, from home and build up a route and routine.. I will aim to do this Every other day at least

I would like to know more about this debilitating condition, not just for me but for my 13 year old son who has also been diagnosed with iih. I have other chronic conditions and it makes me feel very isolated and like I should act 'normal' and that everything is okay, even though it isn’t xx
I am so grateful for my wonderful supportive husband, who really would do anything for me.

@laura174 this is such a lovely goal, and your pugs look adorable! 🐾🤩🐾 Building it up gradually sounds really realistic, and I love that you’ve already thought about what might help on tougher days. With a confidence of 5, maybe there’s one small step that could nudge it to a 6 — even a short, gentle walk counts. And a warm drink or bath afterwards sounds like the perfect reward!☕🛀💛
That’s so lovely to read @laura174!  💛 Having someone who feels truly supportive and in your corner can make such a difference.
This is a great activity, you can really lose yourself by your stream of leaves, and I found it very useful. I will definitely do this at least once a day
I am going to do some baking tomorrow(Saturday) on Saturday, in my kitchen at home . I will aim to do this Once a week
@laura174 did you do some baking on Saturday and complete your goal? Like Kristy, I would love to know what you bake? 😊

I am going to reply to my many messages from friends. When I am unwell I shut myself off from the world. on I am going to do it today and tomorrow.. I will aim to do this I will make it a habit(again)
Even though Iv not been well enough to go out, me and my mum did our daily sudoku and messaged each other updates :0)
I would love to be at peace with myself, and understand I cannot plague myself with things that are the past and/or out of my control. My many illnesses being the main one.

I would really like to start journaling, I hear so much positivity surrounding it and that it helps clear your thoughts and feelings.  on At home, in my bedroom so I’m not distracted.. I will aim to do this Daily eventually.

events.description: I am grateful I can see.
To get the clothes put away on Saturday. I will aim to do this Weekly
IIH really makes us realise how precious our sight is. It is a wonderful thing to be grateful for 🙂

I love your reward of cake, yum! I notice your confidence level is 4. Is there some adjustment you could make to help you fulfil your goal? 

Perhaps just putting some clothes away, not all? I empathise with the struggles having IIH brings. Be kind to yourself.

I had a shunt fitted Dec 25 due to fulminant IIH. I nearly lost my vision. Im not going to lie, its been incredibly scary and im not sure I have compleyely got my head around everything yet (pardon the pun), so im here to find some coping mechanisms.
@Kerry_Chalmers-Worth_ That sounds fab. Its so hard to know where to start trying to pick yourself up again!! Cognitively and emotionally!! X
This disease is so tough x
Thats so nice to hear. X

events.description: I haven’t met anyone with IIH so I want to feel less isolated and to gain knowledge on how to manage the condition and the anxieties that come with it.

I am grateful for my 2 young boys. My eldest shown me a heart with his fingers and said that was for his mummy and daddy and it made me feel warm and loved on the inside and proud of my little boy. My youngest is just about learning to crawl (the wrong direction-ha!) and it made me feel joy, happy and proud.
I’m going to go on a walk on Friday around my Estate. I will aim to do this 1-2 times a week
That's so lovely, children give so much love 😊 Well done to your youngest for beginning to crawl, my boys are teenagers now, time goes so fast.

Seing people online my age do so much and I take it as a win if I can get through work and then feel so exhausted in the evenings I don’t want to do anything

events.description:
I signed up a few months ago after what felt like a whirlwind of getting an unexpected diagnosis of IIH. I was dealing with multiple other major health issues when my papilloedema was picked up at a routine optometrist appointment and I ended up with an emergency hospital admission. It felt like a final straw...

I am here hoping to learn more about how to deal with this condition and to meet other people who have it.
@kristy2 Fellow ADHDer here. I feel like, for me, dealing with managing an illness and its treatment is made so much harder by the executive functioning issues ADHD brings...
@nikki30 Hello. I also have ADHD, and I just wanted to say well done for trying again with the course. Finishing things can be so hard. Good luck!

events.description: I’d like to feel like I understand what I’m facing, that I don’t get lost in the anxiety, loneliness and grief of it all.

replies.text: Hi all, my name is Bethan and I am one of your facilitators for the next 6 weeks.  I'm really looking forward to getting to know you and hear more about why you've joined the programme.  I hope you enjoy taking your first steps 💛.

Hi everyone, I'm Kerry another of your course facilitators. I have IIH myself and as a participant previously found the Hope course very helpful. I'm looking forward to finding out a bit more about you and supporting you though the course journey. I really hope you enjoy the first week of the programme 😊

I joined the course because I want to actively feel like I'm in control. I want to get the things in my life back that feel I've lost. I need guidance and support from others who understand the obstacles we face. My employer treats me like I'm ill but I'm not I have a disability not an illness that's how I see IIH. I'm here to learn how to support myself.

Hi, I'm Amanda and I am one of your facilitators. I live with IIH and have been involved in the setting up of the HOPE course and as a participant. I am looking forward to getting to know you all over the next 6 weeks and supporting you on your HOPE journey. 😊

@lizzie10 Hi Lizzie, Thank you for sharing why you are taking part in the HOPE programme. If you are able to join the HOPE cafe (there are 3 for this course) they are a really good opportunity to talk to other people with IIH and also to the facilitators of the course. We will be looking at gratitude and goal setting which can be really positive tools. Amanda 😊

Hi I’m Jo, I joined because I need to gain some confidence back. I was diagnosed 9 months ago and lost so much but am now starting to take control back and rebuilding my new life. IIH was such a shock and I had so little information on what it was, I got quite good at researching and found support groups online and shared experiences with others and found this really helped. It was the depression that followed diagnosis that made me realise I needed support from others like this Hope Course. Thank you to all of you for providing it to us and I’m looking forward to sharing and learning.

Hi Im Tracey im 47,  I was diagnosed in December last year, for me its triggered some real health anxieties, I had a hysterectomy at 24 as an emergency and a real rare case, my dad has really rare illness he's the only one in the uk , I saved his life when he had a cardiac arrest a couple of years ago and I think having the urgency of your not leaving the hospital following seeing the ophthalmologist, its really triggered something. I feel really annoyed that I feel so much worst since starting medication. I think the struggle is everyone saying it's just a headache. I can feel myself avoiding things for fear of the what ifs and ive been there before and really don't want to be back in the place so im  looking forward to the Hope course and hope that it can be a positive step in taking control.

@joanna39 A warm welcome Joanna. the Hope course is a great place to discover strategies that can help you self-manage, some will resonate with you more than others, which is good as you can tailor your Hope experience to fit with you. It's also a great place to meet others and share experiences, in the forum and during our informal, relaxed cafes.

Hiya Everyone, im Natalie, I joined the hope programme as after being diagnosed over 23 years ago, there still isnt enough information regarding this condition and even now 23 years of having to deal with situations, can still be very overwhelming, just hoping to be able to offer some support and also learn new things. Thank you to everyone.

@tracey69 Hi Tracey, I am sorry to hear that you have had such a tough time with your own health and your dads health. It is really frustrating when people dismiss the IIH headache and don't understand what it is like. If you are able to join the HOPE cafe these are a great opportunity to meet and talk to other people with IIH who get it. Amanda

Hello everyone I’m Rosemary. I’m 30 years old and was diagnosed with IIH in 2023. I had never heard of IIH before so have done some research and joined support group to try and help me understand but I still don’t really understand if the way I feel are symptoms of IIH so it would be nice to have a better understanding and speak to people with the same condition to help me understand it a bit better.

Hi, I’m Bryony. I’m 36 and have had IIH since 2011. I’ve tried all sorts of treatments over the years and have a shunt. I joined the course as I always feel lonely and low. I’m hoping to learn how to live again and be around more people that understand this condition.

Hi, I’m Brie and I’m 32. I have just been officially diagnosed with IIH in February. I had been experiencing symptoms for almost three years but had given up on finding the root cause of them until an opticians visit for new contact lenses flagged it. I joined this course to be able to learn tools for managing living with IIH and also connect with those who have been on this journey that can relate to the challenges I’ve faced.

Hi, my name is Amanda, I am 40 yrs old. I have been diagnosed with IIH for 12 years now. I have an LP shunt which has been fine (mostly) until Nov 18th last year, when it malfunctioned. Long story short I ended up having 3 surgeries in 3 months and I am now writing this from a hospital bed as I'm back  in again since Friday with pressure issues again! I'm waiting to see what they plan to do! I joined the course to learn some techniques to cope and also meet people in a similar position.

@natalie62 Hi Natalie, welcome in, it's great to have you here. Here's hoping that the information shared in the weekly sessions will provide you with more information to support you to manage situations ahead.  Enjoy taking part and the Forum is a great place to reach out to each other.

@rosemary16, welcome to Hope, you've come to the right  place.  Thank you for sharing your story and for joining the conversation.  We look at IIH symptoms in more detail in Session 3 and we share real life case stories throughout which might support your understanding.   Enjoy taking part in the activities and reaching out to others.

@bryony7, thank you for sharing your experience, thoughts and feelings.   It sounds like the course has come at the right time for you.  Hope is a supportive space where you can connect with others and pick up useful tools to keep moving forward.  Gratitude is always a good place to start.

@brie1, welcome to your very own online community of Hope 🍵 .  You are not alone. Hope is a great place to connect with others, learn together and share lots of valuable tools for coping.  I'm looking forward to seeing what you take away each week and you can always tell us more in the end of session feedback.

@amanda85, welcome in.  I'm sorry to hear you've had a tough time of it recently but it's great to have you here, albeit from your hospital bed.  Here's hoping you'll be back home soon with a plan and that the techniques shared each week will provide some support. It also sounds like you will get a lot out of being on the programme with others and you can always reach out to us, your facilitators via messages.  Take care.
I joined the course in the main to help me to learn coping strategies.
@ian43 this is a great place to find strategies that can help you manage your daily life better. 😊

Hi everyone and thank you all as your honesty and openness has encouraged me to post too (bit daunting). This is my second try at the Hope Programme which itself holds some guilt because I took the spot that someone else may have been able to benefit from. `I think I wasn’t in the right headspace to be able to put the effort in… so to new beginnings… I was diagnosed in 2021 because of a CSF leak after nearly 6 years of persisting ironically because I was worried I was loosing my hearing. I haven’t felt like myself for such a long time and I just want to be and feel a bit more like my old me again. I need to make it happen so am going to do my absolute best this time round.

Hi All, I'm Emma.
I found out I had IIH about 2 years ago after a 5 weeks headache I couldn't shift. I tried my doctors a couple times with no real help before trying the opticians on the off chance it was my glasses even though that didn't feel right. When the trainee optician said they wanted me to go to A&E on a Saturday evening I wasn't too impressed and rather confused. I got passed about from A&E to minor injuries to eye casualty and back before eventually spending most of a week in hospital while they worked it all out. 
After all that I just sort of "kept calm and carried on" taking my medication and mostly being OK, but I get very tired and don't have energy I used to. Between that and the brain fog, some days aren't great even if I'm not in pain. 
I joined the course to hopefully meet others with the same problems and find some coping methods. Or just encouragement of knowing it's not just me.

@mikki2 a warm welcome back to Hope. 😊 Thank you for sharing your journey with us, it sounds like you have been through some really difficult times. You will find lots of strategies here that you can try to help you manage your daily life.
@emma205 a warm welcome to Hope. 😊 This is a great place to discover strategies that can help you manage your daily life, plus meet others who have all experience of IIH.

Hello, I am Amy I am 28. I have been diagnosed since April 2020 and I have joined because I recently had to drop out of university to study nursing which has always been my dream due to IIH and my other health conditions so I am hoping to find some new ways to cope and spend some time working on myself.

@amy74 Hi Amy, Welcome to the course. It sounds like the course has come at the right time for you. If you are able to join the HOPE cafe it is is a really good opportunity to meet other people. 😊 Amanda

Hi All. I'm Sinead, I'm 35yrs old and diagnosed with IIH in February 2025 after the optometrist noticed a change in my optic discs at an annual check up. I had thought my headaches and tinnitus were symptoms of other health conditions, so having IIH is still a shock. Still trying to get my head around and what it means for the future. Looking forward to engaging in this course and community.

@sinead4 welcome to the HOPE programme. I am sorry that the diagnosis was a shock. It sounds like the HOPE programme is happening at the right time for you and I hope that you will find the information on the course helps you with the diagnosis and finding strategies that work for you. Amanda 😊

Hi everyone, I'm Olivia. I am 23 years old and was diagnosed with IIH 5 months ago. I found out I had this condition after going to Specsavers. I've had a bit of a rubbish time recently, and haven't been able to do the course properly. Although I am really disappointed, I am catching up now. I joined the course because I needed some support. I want to learn how to manage everything a bit better. I've lost a lot of confidence and I think this course will help me with management and becoming more myself again. I also think that sometimes I feel quite alone so reading comments from others who are in similar situations sounds like something that could help me.

@olivia01 Welcome to the course Olivia and it was lovely to meet you at HOPE cafe today. Please don't be disappointed that you have not been able to start until now and there is still plenty of time to access the materials. We also say to everyone who takes part that sometimes IIH and life just gets in the way. Everyone is welcome to come back and do the course another time in the future. I have done it twice already and have taken away different things each time. I hope that you find things in the course that help you manage your IIH. Amanda 😊
Because being in chronic pain 24/7 is taking its toll and I feel like I’m at the end of my tether

A warm welcome to Hope. Your feelings are valid. Many other IIH  sufferers will have had similar feelings, I certainly have. 

Hope provides some helpful strategies that you can use in daily life to help you manage living with IIH. Hope is also a safe place where you can share experiences with others who relate by having IIH themselves. 

I shall look forward to seeing your Hope journey unfold. 😊
I haven’t received a lot of information from health professionals and I wanted to know ways I could help myself

IIH has led to big changes in my life and how I view myself.
I’m hoping the Hope course will help me find the best route through this.

I feel that my life has been ripped from underneath me and I just don't recognise myself since my diagnosis. A lot of professionals or family I speak to think that all I need to do is lose weight and I will be better and don't seem to understand quite how much this is impacting me. I am hoping I will gain a lot from hearing other people's stories and find some ways to cope and maybe even enjoy life again

@toni7 a warm welcome to the Hope programme. Sadly your experience is common. 

However this Hope programme has been ailored especially for people experiencing the challenges IIH poses. You will find plenty of information and strategies that you may find useful to aid you in your daily life.
Information is unclear and confusing. I feel alone and like no one understands me or my diagnosis including myself.

I feel alone also I have amazing family and partner but it’s changed my personality I don’t want to socialise any more my anxiety is so bad plus the pain and vision issues I just want to hear other peoples experiences so maybe I can feel a bit more normal and reassured

I feel alone also I have amazing family and partner but it’s changed my personality I don’t want to socialise any more my anxiety is so bad plus the pain and vision issues I just want to hear other peoples experiences so maybe I can feel a bit more normal and reassured

Hi 👋 
I also feel my journey with IIH has changed me and how I look at life and others around me. I’ve gone from hard working nurse Jo  to hide away and quiet   Jo. I’ve kept to my tiny world even when friends have put a hand out to help, it’s like I’m ok on my own with IIH but outside in the busy world I just can’t do it.
I feel exactly the same 😘

@ruth39 A warm welcome to the Hope programme. I am certain many others here, including myself have similar experiences. 

Hope is full of different strategies that can help with daily life whilst living with IIH. I look forward to finding out which you find helpful as the course progresses.

@hannah69  A warm welcome to help, your feelings are very valid and I am certain are shared by many others here. I have had simiar experiences myself. 

The Hope programme is filled with straegies to help you through your journey with IIH. The forum is a great place to connect with others on the course, feel free to post there anytime.

@jodymatt your feelings are valid and I am certain shared by many people here. The Hope programme provides loads of strategies for you to try to help in your daily life. 

However if your anxiety is really disabling I would recommend seeking proffessional help.

@joanna51 Your feelings are valid and I am sure many people here relate. The Hope programme provides many straegies your can try to help you. Also it is a safe space to chat to others and find out what helps them during their journey with IIH.
@jodymatt your feelings are valid, I hope that finding others feel similar is reasuring.

@jodymatt you are in the right place to share and hear other peoples experiences of living with IIH. I totally get how isolating it can feel having a rare condition. As part of the HOPE programme there are HOPE cafe which are very informal online sessions where you can chat with the facilitators about the programme and life with IIH. These are also great opportunities to meet other people with IIH and feel that you are not alone. You should have received information about the HOPE cafe dates but I will also pin these on the newsfeed as a reminder with the links. Amanda ☺
Like you all, struggling and need to refocus.

@tunde1 Hi Sorry to hear that you are struggling. The Hope programme has lots of useful strategies that I hope you will find helpful. Do reach out and come along to the HOPE Cafe if you are available. Its a great opportunity to talk to others with IIH and ask questions. Please do put any areas that you are struggling with in particular in the discussion forum and we can share ideas and strategies. Amanda

I have joined the HOPE programme because, like others have mentioned, dealing with IIH can feel overwhelming and isolating. I attended the IIH conference in June and found it extremely helpful, especially the talk by one of the neurologists about research on migraines and IIH. The research he shared literally changed my life, as it informed a medication approach that has made a huge difference for me. It was also wonderful to interact with others who truly understand what this condition feels like. Through this programme, I hope to gain a sense of community and connection, and to engage with others who share similar experiences.

I've joined the programme having been recently diagnosed as I've been struggling to come to terms with living with IHH and the changes in my life. I've also not had much help from medical professionals aside from being told to lose weight (which so far hasn't helped) so I've been feeling a little lost. Hoping that the programme can give me a a bit of connection and help me feel less isolated.

I am recently diagnosed with IIH. Some of the symptoms I have had for a long time but they had worsened. I am experiencing a lot of side effects from the medication and I have felt a lot worse since I received my diagnosis. I feel like giving up on the medication but I am worried about the impact on my vision. At the moment I don't have much hope and have been feeling very negative. I want to change that.

@olivia.g I totally agree with you and your feelings of overwhelm and isolation are echoed across the IIH community. The conference was fabulous this year and so glad you were able to come. The migraine talk was amazing as you say and I am so happy that this has helped you in managing your own condition.  Keep an eye on the IIH UK social media pages as we are talking to venues for next year. ☺I look forward to sharing your HOPE journey with you.  Amanda

@bethanie3 Bethanie everything you say is so valid and I hope that this HOPE course will help you with managing your IIH. We have the first HOPE IIH Cafe this week if you are able to make it as they are a great opportunity to talk to others living with the condition and meet your facilitators Kerry and I who both live with IIH too. Please do reach out to us as well with any questions that you may have. I look forward to connecting with you. Amanda ☺

@helen44 I am so sorry that you are feeling negative and are struggling with side effects of the meds. There are some leaflets on the IIH UK website on each of the medications which you may find useful I will add a link to the discussion forum for you. We have the first HOPE cafe this week which is a great opportunity to connect with others with IIH and ask any questions you may have. It is also a great chance to meet your facilitators Kerry and I who both live with IIH. Do also reach our to us with any questions as well we are here to support you on your HOPE journey. Amanda ☺

I feel like I am the only person I know with IIH. It's coming up to almost two years now and everything still feels like a slog; some days are like wading through mud. I totally get the side effects of medication - everything was stable and then for whatever reason in April it wasn't and we had to re-adjust everything.

I guess I just wanted to be able to communicate with people who understands what it feels like, the tiredness and having to drag yourself through life!

It's going to sound weird, but just reading all your comments above has helped. Not that I am pleased or anything about what you are going through, more that I am reading and going "That's me too - I am not making it up"

@emma223 I am certain many others here (including myself) relate to how you are feeling. The forum is a great place to chat with others on the course. Feel free to start a topic anytime, anything at all. Something as simple as "how is everyone feeling today?"

@emma223 I am so pleased that reading the experiences of others has helped you feel less isolated. If you are free, I would recommend coming along to the Hope cafe on Thursday. Even if it's simply to listen,  obviously, we welcome you joining in too. 🙂

I have joined the programme for many of the reasons others have already said. 
I feel like my life has been turned upside down, and am finding living with IIH very overwhelming and difficult. Was diagnosed almost a year ago, but had symptoms for over a year before that which was 1st treated as a vestibular disorder, which just made me feel worse. And have had very little support or advice about IIH from health professionals since being diagnosed. I used to work full time and study on the side and that's all stopped, people I thought were friends have disappeared since I no longer work and socialise with them and my anxiety is the worst it's ever been. And as much as my family try to support and help me it's hard for them to understand when they know very little about the condition.

Guess I'm hoping the course will help me feel less negative and also connect with people who understand.

I worked and studied full-time just before my diagnosis too. I had just got my PhD and was all engaged with the academic world, then all of a sudden I was having to pace myself in a way I didn't before. People treated me differently and I just couldn't deal with it. I am still trying to develop strategies to help me study/research.

@emma223 
I'm sorry to hear you're experiencing this too, it's so frustrating to not be able to do the things you did before or things you want to do, isn't it! As awful as it sounds it is comforting to know there are others experiencing the same issues and understand what it's like.
Because everyone around me acts like this isn't real and I feel extremely alone.

@donna62 Donna thank you for sharing your experiences with us and I am sorry that you are finding living with IIH overwhelming and difficult. You are definitely in the right place and I hope that you will find information and strategies on the HOPE programme that will support you. If you are able to join the HOPE cafe that is on tomorrow you may find it useful to connect with others with IIH and ask any questions you may have. If you are unable to join the cafe and have any questions relating to information about IIH please do post in the discussion forum or message Kerry and I. We are here to connect with you and support you please do reach out. Amanda ☺

@Gemma's.IIH.Journey Gemma I am so sorry that you feel alone and those around you act like it isn't real. Explaining IIH to those around you is something I know many people find difficult. There are leaflets on the IIH UK website that you may find useful? I will pop a link in the discussion forum for everyone. If you can join the HOPE cafe tomorrow its a really great opportunity to meet others with IIH and to ask any questions you may have. Amanda ☺
